{"id":"project-LICHEN-73i6","title":"SRH write/parse functions produce incomplete RFC 6554 wire format","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c:83-113, 115-154

The SRH (Source Routing Header) write and parse functions handle data starting at the routing-type byte (byte 2 of the RFC 6554 header), missing the first 2 bytes:
- Byte 0: Next Header  
- Byte 1: Hdr Ext Len

The write function documentation says 'Encode SRH to wire format' but produces only 6+16*n bytes instead of the full 8+16*n bytes per RFC 6554.

While the parse documentation correctly states 'starting at routing-type byte', this asymmetry is confusing and could cause interoperability issues.

Suggested fix:
1. Either add Next Header and Hdr Ext Len to write (breaking change), or
2. Clarify documentation for write to match parse ('outputs data starting at routing-type byte')
3. Consider adding a full-header variant for RFC compliance","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:52:43.809314Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:52:43.809349Z"}

1. Either add Next Header and Hdr Ext Len to write (breaking change), or
2. Clarify documentation for write to match parse ('outputs data starting at routing-type byte')
3.
